log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, and it has been detected as potentially malicious. It's also interesting that it has tags related to CVE-2009-1535 and exploit. I'd like to see what types of IOCs are
The sample doesn't seem to have dropped any files, created any processes or mutexes, or used any highlighted API calls. However, it did have 2 unique domains and 2 HTTP conversations. I'd like to see
The sample made DNS lookups for ip.taobao.com and www.yhqdashi.com, and made HTTP conversations with these domains. It also communicated with 106.15.26.124 and 114.55.183.62. I'd like to see what the
The sample didn't create any persistence keys in the registry. However, it did create some other registry keys. I'd like to see what those keys are.
It seems like the 'key_type' argument is not valid for this tool. I'd like to see the actual registry keys that were created.
It seems like the tool is still not providing the actual registry keys. I'd like to see what files were dropped by the sample.
The sample didn't drop any files. I'd like to see what files were written to disk.

Instructions:
